title: Final verification: grep for remaining old token references
description:
## Task
Run a comprehensive grep across the entire codebase to confirm zero remaining old/flat token references in production code. Check for:

1. `var(--deep-navy)` in any .css or .tsx file (excluding tokens.css :root definitions)
2. `var(--cool-gray)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
3. `var(--ocean-blue)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
4. `var(--coral)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
5. `var(--mint-fresh)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
6. `var(--warm-gold)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
7. `var(--light-gray)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
8. `var(--warm-white)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
9. `var(--surface-primary)` without color- prefix (excluding tokens.css :root)
10. `var(--border-subtle)` without color- prefix (excluding tokens.css :root)
11. `var(--text-primary)` without color- prefix
12. `var(--text-secondary)` without color- prefix
13. Hardcoded hex: #1A1A2E, #6B7280, #E8EFF5, #1A2B3D, #64748B in .css/.tsx (excluding tokens.css :root and dark-mode-test.css)

Report findings. If any remain, flag them as gaps.

## Test Steps
1. Each grep pattern returns 0 matches in production code
2. Only allowed exceptions: tokens.css :root brand definitions, dark-mode-test.css intentional test values
3. Run `pnpm run types` - passes
4. Run `pnpm run dev` - no console errors about undefined CSS variables

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

title: Update tokens.css utility classes to semantic token names
description:
## Task
In `src/design-system/tokens.css`, update the utility classes at the bottom of the file to use semantic token names instead of flat names:

- `.heading-5xl` through `.heading-lg`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.body-lg`, `.body-md`, `.body-sm`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.label-lg`, `.label-md`, `.label-sm`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.caption`: change `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`
- `.text-primary`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.text-secondary`: change `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`
- `.status-badge`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)` and `background: var(--light-gray)` → `background: var(--color-surface-secondary)`
- `.status-badge--pending`: `color: var(--warm-gold)` → `color: var(--color-status-warning)`
- `.status-badge--completed`: `color: var(--mint-fresh)` → `color: var(--color-status-success)`
- `.status-badge--cancelled`: `color: var(--coral)` → `color: var(--color-status-error)`
- `.info-box--warning`: `border-color: var(--warm-gold)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-warning)`
- `.info-box--error`: `border-color: var(--coral)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-error)`
- `.info-box--success`: `border-color: var(--mint-fresh)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-success)`
- `.text-order-number`: `color: var(--ocean-blue)` → `color: var(--color-action-primary)`
- `.btn--outline:hover`: `border-color: var(--ocean-blue)` → `border-color: var(--color-action-primary)`
- Input placeholder: `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`

## Test Steps
1. Open tokens.css and verify no `var(--deep-navy)`, `var(--cool-gray)`, `var(--ocean-blue)`, `var(--coral)`, `var(--mint-fresh)`, `var(--warm-gold)`, `var(--light-gray)` remain in utility classes
2. Run `pnpm run types` - passes
3. Visual check: headings, body text, badges, buttons still render correctly

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

title: Update patterns.md with semantic token naming convention
description:
## Task
Update `src/design-system/patterns.md` to document the three-tier semantic token system:

1. Add a section explaining the three-tier architecture: Brand → Alias → Component
2. Document the naming convention:
   - Colors: `--color-{category}-{variant}` (e.g., `--color-text-primary`, `--color-action-primary`, `--color-surface-secondary`)
   - Font sizes: `--font-size-{scale}` (e.g., `--font-size-sm`, `--font-size-xl`) — no shorthand `--text-sm`
   - Motion: `--duration-{speed}`, `--ease-out`
3. List all semantic aliases with their purpose
4. Note that brand-layer tokens (--deep-navy, --ocean-blue, etc.) still exist in :root but should NOT be used directly in components — always use the semantic alias layer
5. Reference `tokens-three-tier.json` as the source of truth

## Test Steps
1. Read updated patterns.md - naming convention is clear and complete
2. All documented token names match what's defined in tokens.css
3. No references to old flat token usage patterns remain in the docs
4. Examples use semantic names, not flat names

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

title: Get bearings for Migrate dark mode test page CSS to semantic tokens
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Convert dark-mode-test.css from hardcoded hex values to semantic tokens. Update DarkModeTestUI.tsx if it has inline styles with old tokens.

Test Steps:
1. Visit /dark-mode-test page
2. Toggle dark mode - all test swatches should adapt via tokens
3. Compare against expected token values

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)
3. **Smoke test via dev-browser** - Navigate to the app and verify a core flow works
   - Use the /dev-browser skill
   - Pick a completed feature from recent work
   - Verify it still functions correctly

### If Health Check FAILS

If ANY of these fail, you CANNOT proceed with this task. Report back to orchestrator:

1. **Identify the cause** - Check git log for recent changes:
   ```bash
   git log --oneline -5
   ```
   Commits should reference bead IDs (e.g., "Refs: beads-xxx")

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.
   The orchestrator will handle creating bug beads and blocking.

## STEP 2: Task-Specific Exploration

Once health check passes:

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files
2. **Read key files** - Understand existing patterns and conventions
3. **Identify change locations** - Where will modifications be needed?
4. **Note dependencies** - What other code might be affected?
5. **Document findings** - Add a comment summarizing what you learned

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with your findings, then report to orchestrator.

**If health checks passed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTHY
FILES: <key files that will be modified>
PATTERNS: <existing patterns to follow>
APPROACH: <recommended implementation approach>
CONCERNS: <any concerns or questions>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.
```

**If health checks failed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED
FAILURE: <what failed - dev server, tests, smoke test>
ERROR: <error message or description>
CAUSE: <likely cause from git log investigation>
RELATED_EPIC: <bead-id from git log if found, or "unknown">

ACTION: You must handle this health check failure:
1. Check for existing bug beads: `bd list --type=bug --status=open`
2. If no existing bug covers this, create one:
   bd --no-daemon mol pour bug-fix --var title="Health check failure: <brief description>" --var task="<details from my report above>" --var related_epic="<RELATED_EPIC value>" --assignee ralph
3. If RELATED_EPIC is a bead ID, link bug to it: `bd dep add <bug-id> <RELATED_EPIC>`
4. Block current epic on the bug: `bd dep add <your-id> <bug-id>`
5. Move current epic back to open: `bd update <your-id> --status open`
6. Close this bearings step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
7. Exit - do not continue with other steps
```

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed (validation, security, integration glue, missing API connections):
```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description with file context>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encountered something noteworthy:
- An error you struggled with and how you fixed it
- A pattern you had to follow that wasn't documented
- A library/component usage gotcha future agents should know
- Something that might warrant a skill (e.g., "always use X when doing Y")

```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

Only add gaps/learnings for non-obvious issues that would help future agents.

title: Wire payment emails to webhook and confirm flow
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Wire payment emails to webhook and confirm flow

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

In webhook checkout.session.completed: send PaymentReceived email to customer after creating Payment record. In confirmOrder: pass checkoutUrl and price breakdown to OrderConfirmed email. Add sendPaymentReceivedEmail to email.ts. Handle missing email gracefully.

## Test Steps
1. Stripe payment triggers PaymentReceived email
2. Email sent to correct customer
3. confirmOrder sends OrderConfirmed with checkout URL
4. OrderConfirmed includes price breakdown
5. Missing email doesn't crash
6. Errors handled gracefully

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Verify TipSelector component
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create src/app/pages/orders/components/TipSelector.tsx ('use client'). Props: onTipChange(tipCents), currentTip. Preset buttons: No Tip, $5, $10, Custom. Custom shows input for dollar amount, converts to cents. Selected button highlighted. Tip NOT in platform fee. Design tokens. Compact horizontal layout.

## Test Steps
1. Preset buttons render correctly
2. Clicking $5 calls onTipChange(500)
3. No Tip calls onTipChange(0)
4. Custom shows input field
5. Custom converts dollars to cents
6. Selected button highlighted
7. One option active at a time
8. Design tokens used

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Task Context
Handle Stripe onboarding return/refresh URLs: Return (/admin/settings/stripe?onboarding=complete) calls checkOnboardingStatus, shows success or pending. Refresh (/admin/settings/stripe?onboarding=refresh) generates new onboarding link. Parse URL params in StripeSettingsUI.

## Test Steps
1. Return with ?onboarding=complete shows success
2. Return triggers checkOnboardingStatus
3. Refresh URL generates new onboarding link
4. Not-yet-complete shows pending message
5. Multiple returns handled idempotently

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format

```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

title: Commit Data migration for existing orders
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Create data migration script: parse existing string price values to cents where possible (e.g. '45' -> 4500, '$45.50' -> 4550). Set unparseable prices to null. Set amountPaid based on old paymentStatus (if was 'paid', set amountPaid = price). Handle null prices. Log results.

## Test Steps
1. Numeric string prices converted correctly
2. Dollar sign prices converted
3. Ranges/text prices set to null
4. Previously paid orders have amountPaid set
5. Migration is idempotent

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment with the commit details:
```
STATUS: DONE
COMMIT: <commit hash>
MESSAGE: <commit message summary>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to next steps (if any).
```

When complete, close this bead with `bd close <this-bead-id>`.

description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Update admin order list and print view payment badges

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Update AdminOrdersUI.tsx and PrintOrdersUI.tsx: payment badges use getPaymentStatus. Badge colors: unpaid=red, partial=yellow, paid=green, overpaid=blue. Print view shows amountPaid/totalDue. Payment filters work with derived status.

## Test Steps
1. Correct color badges per state
2. Unpaid red, partial yellow, paid green
3. Print view shows paid/total
4. Payment filters work
5. No old paymentStatus enum refs

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Verify Create Payment model in Prisma schema
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create new Payment model in prisma/schema.prisma: id String @id @default(uuid()), orderId String (FK to Order), amount Int (cents), method String (stripe|cash|venmo|zelle|other), type String (deposit|payment|tip|refund), stripePaymentId String?, notes String?, createdAt DateTime @default(now()). Add relation to Order model. Add payments Payment[] to Order.

## Test Steps
1. Payment model defined with all fields
2. Relation to Order correct (FK on orderId)
3. Order model has payments Payment[] relation
4. id uses uuid default

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS

TESTS: pass

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
```

title: Implement PaymentActions component
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Create src/app/pages/orders/components/PaymentActions.tsx ('use client'). Props: order (pricing fields), tipAmount, onPay. Derive status via getPaymentStatus. States: 1) unpaid no deposit: Pay $X button. 2) unpaid with deposit: Pay deposit + Pay full buttons. 3) partial: Pay remaining. 4) paid: green checkmark. 5) unconfirmed: nothing. Always show 'or pay at pickup' except paid. useTransition for loading.

## Test Steps

4. Paid shows green status
5. Unconfirmed shows nothing

title: Create Payment model in Prisma schema
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Create Payment model in Prisma schema

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Create new Payment model in prisma/schema.prisma: id String @id @default(uuid()), orderId String (FK to Order), amount Int (cents), method String (stripe|cash|venmo|zelle|other), type String (deposit|payment|tip|refund), stripePaymentId String?, notes String?, createdAt DateTime @default(now()). Add relation to Order model. Add payments Payment[] to Order.

## Test Steps
1. Payment model defined with all fields
2. Relation to Order correct (FK on orderId)
3. Order model has payments Payment[] relation
4. id uses uuid default

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Verify Data migration for existing orders
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create data migration script: parse existing string price values to cents where possible (e.g. '45' -> 4500, '$45.50' -> 4550). Set unparseable prices to null. Set amountPaid based on old paymentStatus (if was 'paid', set amountPaid = price). Handle null prices. Log results.

## Test Steps
1. Numeric string prices converted correctly
2. Dollar sign prices converted
3. Ranges/text prices set to null
4. Previously paid orders have amountPaid set
5. Migration is idempotent

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS
TYPES: pass
TESTS: pass
LINT: pass (or N/A)
UI: pass (or N/A)
SCREENSHOTS: <paths if applicable>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
   - Block yourself: `bd update <your-id> --status blocked`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Exit
```

Add money utilities (src/utils/stripe.ts or src/utils/money.ts): formatCents(cents) returns '$45.00'. parseDollars(input) parses '$45.50' or '45' to 4550/4500. calculatePlatformFee(priceCents, feeBps, feeModel) returns { customerTotal, vendorReceives, platformFee }. feeModel customer: fee on top. vendor: fee from share. split: 50/50. ## Goal

## Task Context

## Test Steps

2. amountPaid decremented correctly

4. account.updated sets onboarding complete when ready

title: Implement Create getPaymentStatus derived helper
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Create src/utils/payments.ts with getPaymentStatus(order): returns 'unpaid'|'deposit'|'partial'|'paid'|'overpaid'|null. Logic: null totalDue -> null, amountPaid==0 -> unpaid, amountPaid > 0 && < totalDue -> partial, >= totalDue -> paid. Export PaymentStatus type.

## Test Steps

title: Verify confirmOrder: deposit logic
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add deposit support to confirmOrder: check org.defaultDepositBps. Accept optional depositOverride (cents or null). Calculate depositAmount: override > org default > null. Store on order. If deposit AND Stripe connected: Checkout session for depositAmount. If no deposit: session for full totalDue.

## Test Steps
1. Org 50% default: depositAmount = totalDue / 2
2. Override of 2000: depositAmount = 2000
3. Override null: no deposit regardless of org default
4. No default, no override: depositAmount null
5. Checkout amount matches deposit when deposit mode

CHANGES: src/api/stripe-webhook.ts — Added handleCheckoutSessionCompleted and handlePaymentIntentSucceeded handlers. Key impl details:
- ensureDb() called since webhook runs before middleware
- Platform metadata validation (fresh-catch)
- Payment dedup via stripePaymentId

description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal

## Task Context
Add money utilities (src/utils/stripe.ts or src/utils/money.ts): formatCents(cents) returns '$45.00'. parseDollars(input) parses '$45.50' or '45' to 4550/4500. calculatePlatformFee(priceCents, feeBps, feeModel) returns { customerTotal, vendorReceives, platformFee }. feeModel customer: fee on top. vendor: fee from share. split: 50/50. All integer cents.

## Test Steps
1. formatCents(4500) returns '$45.00'
2. formatCents(0) returns '$0.00'
3. parseDollars('$45.50') returns 4550

5. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'customer') = { customerTotal: 10500, vendorReceives: 10000, platformFee: 500 }
6. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'vendor') = { customerTotal: 10000, vendorReceives: 9500, platformFee: 500 }
7. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'split') = { customerTotal: 10250, vendorReceives: 9750, platformFee: 500 }

# ORCHESTRATOR: Money utility functions

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Add money utilities (src/utils/stripe.ts or src/utils/money.ts): formatCents(cents) returns '$45.00'. parseDollars(input) parses '$45.50' or '45' to 4550/4500. calculatePlatformFee(priceCents, feeBps, feeModel) returns { customerTotal, vendorReceives, platformFee }. feeModel customer: fee on top. vendor: fee from share. split: 50/50. comments.text:
STATUS: HEALTHY
Dev server starts OK (vite 6.3.5, rwsdk scanning works). No test suite configured.

FILES:
- src/design-system/tokens.css (lines 487-693) — utility classes section
- src/design-system/tokens-three-tier.json — semantic token schema (reference only)
- 6 TSX files use utility classes: CustomerHomeUI, MarketCard, AdminDashboardUI, JoinUI, BottomNavigation.v2, ShareModal

PATTERNS:
Old flat names used in utility classes (lines 492-514): --deep-navy, --cool-gray
Semantic equivalents already defined (lines 176-213): --color-text-primary, --color-text-secondary, --color-text-tertiary
Mapping:
  --deep-navy → --color-text-primary
  --cool-gray → --color-text-secondary
  --ocean-blue → --color-action-primary
  --warm-gold → --color-accent-gold / --color-status-warning
  --mint-fresh → --color-status-success
  --coral → --color-status-error / --color-action-secondary
  --light-gray → --color-surface-secondary
  --warm-white → --color-surface-warm
  --text-primary → --color-text-primary (intermediate alias, still flat)
  --text-secondary → --color-text-secondary

3 areas to update:
1. Typography utility classes (lines 492-514): .heading-*, .body-*, .label-*, .caption, .text-primary, .text-secondary — replace --deep-navy/--cool-gray with --color-text-primary/--color-text-secondary
2. Component utility classes (lines 560-608): .status-badge, .info-box variants — replace --light-gray/--deep-navy/--warm-gold/--mint-fresh/--coral with semantic aliases
3. Additional typography utils (lines 651-678): .text-heading-lg, .text-subheading, .text-order-number, .text-metadata — replace --text-primary/--text-secondary/--ocean-blue with semantic names
4. Placeholder styling (line 398): --cool-gray → --color-text-secondary
5. Button hover styles (lines 267-281): hardcoded rgba values + --ocean-blue → semantic tokens
6. Dark mode placeholder (line 482): --cool-gray → --color-text-secondary

APPROACH:
Pure find-and-replace in tokens.css utility section (lines 487+), placeholder rules (398, 482), and button hover rules (267-281). No structural changes. All semantic tokens already exist in :root. Zero risk of breaking consumers since we're changing CSS var references inside class definitions, not renaming the classes themselves.

CONCERNS:
- Button hover rgba(0,102,204,...) hardcoded values (lines 268, 272, 277, 281) have no direct semantic token equivalent. Could leave as-is or extract new tokens. Recommend leaving hardcoded rgba hover shadows for now, only updating --ocean-blue ref on line 278.
- --text-primary/--text-secondary (lines 157-158) are intermediate aliases already pointing to --deep-navy/--cool-gray. Lines 655/662/676 use these. Should migrate to --color-text-primary/--color-text-secondary.

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.

title: Data migration for existing orders
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Data migration for existing orders

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Create data migration script: parse existing string price values to cents where possible (e.g. '45' -> 4500, '$45.50' -> 4550). Set unparseable prices to null. Set amountPaid based on old paymentStatus (if was 'paid', set amountPaid = price). Handle null prices. Log results.

## Test Steps
1. Numeric string prices converted correctly
2. Dollar sign prices converted
3. Ranges/text prices set to null
4. Previously paid orders have amountPaid set
5. Migration is idempotent

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Verify Fee and deposit config server functions
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add to stripe-functions.ts: updateFeeConfig(orgId, feeBps, feeModel) validates feeBps 0-5000, feeModel customer|vendor|split, updates org. updateDepositConfig(orgId, depositBps) validates 0-10000 or null, updates org. Both require admin access.

## Test Steps
1. updateFeeConfig updates platformFeeBps
2. Updates feeModel
3. Rejects invalid feeBps
4. Rejects invalid feeModel
5. updateDepositConfig sets depositBps
6. Accepts null to disable
7. Rejects non-admin
8. pnpm run types passes

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

```
ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
```

APPROACH: Add webhook handler as middleware entry before render() that intercepts POST /api/stripe/webhook, reads raw body, verifies sig with constructEventAsync+SubtleCryptoProvider, dispatches events.
CONCERNS: stripe package not installed yet (other Ralph working on it) — can still write code that imports it.
ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.

title: confirmOrder: deposit logic
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: confirmOrder: deposit logic

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Add deposit support to confirmOrder: check org.defaultDepositBps. Accept optional depositOverride (cents or null). Calculate depositAmount: override > org default > null. Store on order. If deposit AND Stripe connected: Checkout session for depositAmount. If no deposit: session for full totalDue.

description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create fee preview calculator for settings: sample $50 order (or adjustable). Shows: Customer pays X, Vendor receives Y, Platform fee Z. If deposit: Deposit required. Updates live as config changes. Uses calculatePlatformFee. formatCents. Design tokens.

## Test Steps
1. Correct for customer absorb
2. Correct for vendor absorb
3. Correct for split
4. Updates on fee % change
5. Updates on model change
6. Deposit line when enabled
7. Sample amount adjustable
8. formatCents for all displays

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

```
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
```

title: Fee config route and permissions
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Fee config route and permissions

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Fee config accessible from /admin/settings/stripe. Add nav link from admin dashboard/settings. Admin-only access. Owner-only for fee config if role exists. StripeSettingsPage fetches fee config data for UI.

## Test Steps
1. Accessible from /admin/settings/stripe
2. Non-admin cannot access
3. Nav link visible in admin
4. Server component passes fee data
5. Page loads current values

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

1. Null price shows 'Price pending' or dash
2. Priced orders display correctly

4. Null values sort last

```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

title: Implement Stripe webhook route with signature verification
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Add POST /api/stripe/webhook route in src/worker.tsx BEFORE render() block. Read raw body as Buffer.from(await request.arrayBuffer()). Verify with constructEventAsync + SubtleCryptoProvider. Return 200 on success, 400 on bad signature. Bypass RWSDK middleware. Log event type. Dispatch to handlers.

## Test Steps
1. Valid signature returns 200
2. Invalid signature returns 400
3. Missing signature header returns 400
4. Event type logged
5. Route doesn't interfere with other routes
6. Raw body preserved (not parsed by middleware)

## Instructions

1. **Review bearings notes** - Check the bearings step's comments for context
2. **Make focused changes** - Implement one thing at a time
3. **Follow existing patterns** - Match the codebase style
4. **Keep changes minimal** - Don't refactor unrelated code
5. **Add necessary tests** - If the codebase has tests, add relevant ones

## Best Practices
- Use Edit tool for modifications (not Write for existing files)
- Preserve existing formatting and conventions
- Add comments for complex logic
- Consider edge cases

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead summarizing what was changed, then report to orchestrator.

title: Get bearings for confirmOrder: deposit logic
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal

## Task Context
Add deposit support to confirmOrder: check org.defaultDepositBps. Accept optional depositOverride (cents or null). Calculate depositAmount: override > org default > null. Store on order. If deposit AND Stripe connected: Checkout session for depositAmount. If no deposit: session for full totalDue.

1. Org 50% default: depositAmount = totalDue / 2

title: Verify Webhook: payment success handlers
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Implement webhook handlers: checkout.session.completed -> extract orderId from metadata, create Payment record (amount, method=stripe, type based on deposit/full), increment order.amountPaid, set paidAt if fully paid, store stripePaymentIntentId. payment_intent.succeeded as backup (skip if Payment already exists). Verify platform metadata = fresh-catch.

## Test Steps
1. checkout.session.completed creates Payment record
2. amountPaid incremented correctly
3. Full payment sets paidAt
4. Deposit does NOT set paidAt
5. payment_intent.succeeded works as backup
6. Duplicate events handled idempotently
7. Wrong platform metadata ignored

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS
TYPES: pass
TESTS: pass
LINT: pass (or N/A)
UI: pass (or N/A)
SCREENSHOTS: <paths if applicable>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
```

title: Verify Stripe webhook route with signature verification
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add POST /api/stripe/webhook route in src/worker.tsx BEFORE render() block. Read raw body as Buffer.from(await request.arrayBuffer()). Verify with constructEventAsync + SubtleCryptoProvider. Return 200 on success, 400 on bad signature. Bypass RWSDK middleware. Log event type. Dispatch to handlers.

## Test Steps
1. Valid signature returns 200
2. Invalid signature returns 400
3. Missing signature header returns 400
4. Event type logged
5. Route doesn't interfere with other routes

title: Stripe webhook route with signature verification
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Stripe webhook route with signature verification

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Add POST /api/stripe/webhook route in src/worker.tsx BEFORE render() block. Read raw body as Buffer.from(await request.arrayBuffer()). Verify with constructEventAsync + SubtleCryptoProvider. Return 200 on success, 400 on bad signature. Bypass RWSDK middleware. Log event type. Dispatch to handlers.

title: Verify confirmOrder: numeric price and fee calculation
description:

## Task Context
Update confirmOrder in src/app/pages/admin/order-functions.ts: accept price as cents (not string). Fetch org platformFeeBps and feeModel. Snapshot platformFeeBps on order. Calculate platformFee via calculatePlatformFee. Calculate totalDue: customer=price+fee, vendor=price, split=price+fee/2. Store price, platformFeeBps, platformFee, totalDue. Keep status->confirmed and adminNotes.

## Test Steps
1. Accepts numeric price in cents
2. platformFeeBps snapshotted from org
3. Customer absorb: totalDue = price + fee
4. Vendor absorb: totalDue = price
5. Split: totalDue = price + half fee
6. Status changes to confirmed
7. adminNotes still saved

1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)

- [ ] Code review looks good

   - White-on-white text or poor contrast

title: Fee and deposit config server functions
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Fee and deposit config server functions

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Add to stripe-functions.ts: updateFeeConfig(orgId, feeBps, feeModel) validates feeBps 0-5000, feeModel customer|vendor|split, updates org. updateDepositConfig(orgId, depositBps) validates 0-10000 or null, updates org. Both require admin access.

## Test Steps
1. updateFeeConfig updates platformFeeBps
2. Updates feeModel
3. Rejects invalid feeBps
4. Rejects invalid feeModel
5. updateDepositConfig sets depositBps
6. Accepts null to disable
7. Rejects non-admin
8. pnpm run types passes

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

- L196: var(--deep-navy) → var(--color-text-primary)
- L207: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L213: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L223: var(--surface-primary) → var(--color-surface-primary)
- L228: rgba(100,116,139,0.2) → var(--color-border-medium)
- L230: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L242: var(--deep-navy) → var(--color-text-primary)

MAPPING (hex fallback + old flat → semantic):
#6B7280 / var(--cool-gray)         → var(--color-text-secondary)
#1A1A2E / var(--deep-navy)         → var(--color-text-primary)
#F3F4F6 / var(--light-gray)        → var(--color-surface-secondary)
white / var(--surface-primary)      → var(--color-surface-primary)
#E8EFF5                             → var(--color-border-input) [already defined as #E8EFF5]
#CBD5E1                             → var(--color-border-input-disabled) [already defined as #CBD5E1]
rgba(0,102,204,0.1)                 → var(--color-border-subtle)
rgba(100,116,139,0.1)               → var(--color-border-subtle)
rgba(100,116,139,0.2)               → var(--color-border-medium)

APPROACH: Remove hex fallback lines entirely (the pattern is: hex on one line, var() override on next — just keep the var() line). Replace old flat token refs with semantic aliases. The rgba() border values map to existing semantic border tokens. Total: ~28 replacements across one file.

CONCERNS:
- #1A1A2E != #1A2B3D (deep-navy). The hex #1A1A2E is not the actual deep-navy value. Using var(--color-text-primary) which resolves to var(--deep-navy)=#1A2B3D is a slight color shift but correct per the design system.
- The hex+var() fallback pattern (hex first, then var() override) was for browser compat. Removing the hex line is safe since CSS custom properties have near-universal support now.
- Pre-existing TS errors in JoinUI.tsx (autoFocus prop) are unrelated and won't block this work.

APPROACH: (1) Delete hex fallback lines where var() line exists below, (2) Replace standalone hex values with semantic tokens, (3) Upgrade old flat tokens to semantic names. ~28 edits total.
CONCERNS: #1A1A2E differs slightly from --deep-navy (#1A2B3D) - minor color shift when removing fallback. Pre-existing TS error in JoinUI.tsx unrelated.

title: Verify Money utility functions
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add money utilities (src/utils/stripe.ts or src/utils/money.ts): formatCents(cents) returns '$45.00'. parseDollars(input) parses '$45.50' or '45' to 4550/4500. calculatePlatformFee(priceCents, feeBps, feeModel) returns { customerTotal, vendorReceives, platformFee }. feeModel customer: fee on top. vendor: fee from share. split: 50/50. All integer cents.

## Test Steps
1. formatCents(4500) returns '$45.00'
2. formatCents(0) returns '$0.00'
3. parseDollars('$45.50') returns 4550
4. parseDollars('45') returns 4500
5. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'customer') = { customerTotal: 10500, vendorReceives: 10000, platformFee: 500 }
6. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'vendor') = { customerTotal: 10000, vendorReceives: 9500, platformFee: 500 }
7. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'split') = { customerTotal: 10250, vendorReceives: 9750, platformFee: 500 }

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS
TYPES: pass
TESTS: pass
LINT: pass (or N/A)
UI: pass (or N/A)
SCREENSHOTS: <paths if applicable>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`

   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
```

# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal

## Task Context
Handle Stripe onboarding return/refresh URLs: Return (/admin/settings/stripe?onboarding=complete) calls checkOnboardingStatus, shows success or pending. Refresh (/admin/settings/stripe?onboarding=refresh) generates new onboarding link. Parse URL params in StripeSettingsUI.

## Test Steps

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files

title: Commit Stripe webhook route with signature verification
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Add POST /api/stripe/webhook route in src/worker.tsx BEFORE render() block. Read raw body as Buffer.from(await request.arrayBuffer()). Verify with constructEventAsync + SubtleCryptoProvider. Return 200 on success, 400 on bad signature. Bypass RWSDK middleware. Log event type. Dispatch to handlers.

## Test Steps
1. Valid signature returns 200
2. Invalid signature returns 400
3. Missing signature header returns 400
4. Event type logged
5. Route doesn't interfere with other routes
6. Raw body preserved (not parsed by middleware)

# ORCHESTRATOR: Fee and deposit configuration UI

Add to StripeSettingsUI or separate FeeConfigUI.tsx ('use client'): fee % input (stored as basis points), fee model radio (Customer/Vendor/Split), save button. Deposit toggle + % input. useTransition for saves. Show current values. Success feedback after save. Design tokens.

## Test Steps
1. Fee input shows current value
2. Fee model shows current value
3. Save fee updates org
4. Deposit toggle reflects setting
5. Enable deposit shows % input
6. Save deposit updates org
7. Loading states during save
8. Success message after save

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Verify Fee config route and permissions
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Fee config accessible from /admin/settings/stripe. Add nav link from admin dashboard/settings. Admin-only access. Owner-only for fee config if role exists. StripeSettingsPage fetches fee config data for UI.

## Test Steps
1. Accessible from /admin/settings/stripe
2. Non-admin cannot access
3. Nav link visible in admin
4. Server component passes fee data
5. Page loads current values

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

title: Commit Create getPaymentStatus derived helper
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Create src/utils/payments.ts with getPaymentStatus(order): returns 'unpaid'|'deposit'|'partial'|'paid'|'overpaid'|null. Logic: null totalDue -> null, amountPaid==0 -> unpaid, amountPaid > 0 && < totalDue -> partial, >= totalDue -> paid. Export PaymentStatus type.

## Test Steps
1. null totalDue returns null
2. amountPaid 0, totalDue 4500 returns 'unpaid'
3. amountPaid 2000, totalDue 4500 returns 'partial'
4. amountPaid 4500, totalDue 4500 returns 'paid'
5. amountPaid 5000, totalDue 4500 returns 'overpaid'
6. PaymentStatus type exported

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

```
<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

- 5 local Order type defs: price string|null -> number|null (AdminOrdersUI, PrintOrdersUI, CustomerOrdersUI, OrderCard, AdminOrderCard)
- order-functions.ts: confirmOrder accepts number instead of string
- AdminOrderCard: price input converts dollars->cents, display formats cents->dollars

title: Commit Webhook: refund and account handlers
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Implement: charge.refunded -> create Payment(amount negative, method=stripe, type=refund), decrement amountPaid, clear paidAt if now under totalDue. account.updated -> find org by stripeAccountId, check charges_enabled AND details_submitted, update stripeOnboardingComplete.

## Test Steps
1. charge.refunded creates negative Payment record
2. amountPaid decremented correctly
3. Full refund resets paidAt
4. account.updated sets onboarding complete when ready
5. account.updated keeps incomplete when not ready
6. Unknown account ID handled gracefully

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment with the commit details:
```
STATUS: DONE
COMMIT: <commit hash>
MESSAGE: <commit message summary>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to next steps (if any).
```

When complete, close this bead with `bd close <this-bead-id>`.

title: Implement Fee and deposit config server functions
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Add to stripe-functions.ts: updateFeeConfig(orgId, feeBps, feeModel) validates feeBps 0-5000, feeModel customer|vendor|split, updates org. updateDepositConfig(orgId, depositBps) validates 0-10000 or null, updates org. Both require admin access.

## Test Steps
1. updateFeeConfig updates platformFeeBps
2. Updates feeModel
3. Rejects invalid feeBps
4. Rejects invalid feeModel
5. updateDepositConfig sets depositBps
6. Accepts null to disable
7. Rejects non-admin
8. pnpm run types passes

## Instructions

1. **Review bearings notes** - Check the bearings step's comments for context
2. **Make focused changes** - Implement one thing at a time
3. **Follow existing patterns** - Match the codebase style
4. **Keep changes minimal** - Don't refactor unrelated code
5. **Add necessary tests** - If the codebase has tests, add relevant ones

## Best Practices
- Use Edit tool for modifications (not Write for existing files)
- Preserve existing formatting and conventions
- Add comments for complex logic
- Consider edge cases
